TwinPage.title: TWIN
TwinPage.subtitle: A standalone test app where language and region are selected independently. The default journey is English language with Kenya-specific content.

spotlight.title: Language is not the same as region
spotlight.description: This page uses next-intl for translations, while region-specific details are chosen separately. A user can browse in English and still receive content tailored to Kenya.

ke.name: Kenya
ke.headline: Kenya-specific operating context
ke.summary: Content emphasizes East African trade flows, local pilot relevance, and institutions that matter for rollout in Kenya.

global.name: Global
global.headline: Cross-border core value proposition
global.summary: Content is framed around international trade data exchange, document authenticity, and reusable digital infrastructure across markets.

signals.eastAfricanCorridor: Focus on the East African trade corridor and Kenya-linked flows through ports and customs checkpoints.
signals.publicPrivatePartners: Highlight collaboration patterns with public institutions and organizations such as Trademark Africa.
signals.smeExporters: Prioritize explainers that help SMEs and exporters understand faster processing and lower friction.
signals.crossBorderData: Explain how structured trade data can move reliably across multiple jurisdictions.
signals.documentAuthenticity: Stress authenticity, traceability, and controlled access to trade documents.
signals.openNetworkDesign: Frame TWIN as reusable digital infrastructure rather than a single-country deployment.

mombasaPort.title: Port and corridor visibility
mombasaPort.description: Describe a Kenya-facing pilot around shipment milestones, document handovers, and trusted data exchange connected to Mombasa-linked flows.

tradeFinance.title: SME trade finance handoff
tradeFinance.description: Position TWIN as a way to reduce friction for exporters by sharing verifiable digital trade records with banks and counterparties.

certificateFlow.title: Certificate validation workflow
certificateFlow.description: Show how digital certificates can be verified faster, reducing manual checks and delays in cross-border trade processing.

documentTokenization.title: Document tokenization
documentTokenization.description: Explain how important trade documents can be represented digitally while preserving controlled access and authenticity.

crossBorderVisibility.title: Cross-border data visibility
crossBorderVisibility.description: Showcase the value of a shared data layer for status updates, provenance, and trusted collaboration across countries.

adaptation.title: How the content is adapted
adaptation.description: The translation layer answers what language the user sees. The region layer answers which market signals, pilots, and local context should be highlighted.

points.englishUi: The interface stays in English for a Kenyan user who prefers English.
points.kenyanWorkflows: Examples and proof points are selected to match Kenyan trade and logistics workflows.
points.localPartnerships: Local partner references are surfaced without changing the app language.
points.languageFallbacks: The same regional profile can be rendered in multiple languages as translations become available.
points.regionalContentRules: Regional content is selected by region code rather than by locale.
points.sharedCorePlatform: Core product messaging stays reusable while market-specific context changes.

crowdin.title: Why this structure works well with Crowdin
crowdin.pointOne: All translatable UI and market copy is stored in locale-based JSON message files.
crowdin.pointTwo: Region logic lives in a separate data model, so translators do not need to encode market rules.
crowdin.pointThree: Adding a new locale mostly means supplying a new messages file without changing the region catalog.
